name: Jimmy Maxwell
abstract: Jimmy Maxwell (January 9, 1917 – July 20, 2002) was an American swing jazz trumpeter. Maxwell played cornet from an early age, studying with Herbert L.

quotes.text: Sonny, yeah. Anything you'd need. Yeah, Sonny was a nice guy. My wife was furious at me because one night I had some tickets to a Duke Ellington concert and I brought my daughter in for dinner. And all of a sudden I had to stay later, so I said, 'Sonny, will you take my daughter to Carnegie Hall?' And my wife said, 'who's that man who brought Anne to Carnegie Hall?' I said, Sonny. She said, 'Sonny's a
quotes.caption: Sonny brought his daughter to a Duke Ellington concert

quotes.text: thief?" I said, yeah. She said, "you sent your daughter to Carnegie Hall with a thief?" I said, he's a nice guy, you know. I don't know he's a thief; just people say he's a thief.
quotes.caption: Anecdote about a daughter's concern regarding a 'thief' at Carnegie Hall

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Andre
about_quotes.evidence: Trumpets were Jimmy Maxwell, Joe Newman, Joe Wilder and Johnny Frosk. ... Trombone were Jimmy Knepper, Willie Dennis and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder
about_quotes.evidence: So the band was very well integrated, a tremendous bunch of fellows: Zoot Sims, Jimmy Maxwell, Johnny Frosk.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.evidence: So Jimmy Maxwell played in some rehearsal bands that I was in, and we got to be really good friends.

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Rosengarden
about_quotes.evidence: I had Snooky Young, Jimmy Maxwell, a wonderful trumpet player... I brought in a lot of good players.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Rosengarden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 6, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Crow
about_quotes.evidence: I was working with Joe Wilder at that time, and Wayne Andre and Jimmy Maxwell.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Crow interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Vax
about_quotes.evidence: he called Conte Candoli and Jimmy Maxwell and said, “I want you to look out for my kid Mike.” ... they needed a fourth trumpet player for the faculty band for the Kenton band. And so here I am this young kid and they put me in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Vax interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 29, 2022
